danger_signals.visual_form: 3D-rendered creature sprites in first-person dungeon view
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; enemies visible in dungeon corridors trigger combat engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Blocked passages, cave-in debris in dungeon architecture
danger_signals.meaning: Structural danger; Xanathar's collapse blocks retreat and forces progression deeper

danger_signals.visual_form: Drow and Dwarf clan enemies encountered in descending levels
danger_signals.meaning: Territorial threats; different dungeon depths controlled by hostile groups

safety_signals.visual_form: Four-character party roster (expandable to six via NPCs/resurrections)
safety_signals.meaning: Safety through numbers; larger party reduces vulnerability in dungeon

safety_signals.visual_form: Non-hostile NPCs encountered throughout dungeons available for recruitment
safety_signals.meaning: Potential allies; can strengthen party and provide tactical advantages

safety_signals.visual_form: Descent through dungeon levels to Xanathar's lair and final confrontation
safety_signals.meaning: Goal clarity; moving toward definable objective signals strategic safety

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Surface city location; initiates player quest through lords hiring adventurers

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-level beholder enemy with distinct visual identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Lair in deepest dungeon level; associated with cave collapses and sewer control

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dwarf enemies encountered in mid-dungeon levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dwarf-controlled dungeon territories; carved dwarf architecture in specific levels

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Drow enemies with distinct dark elf visual characteristics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Drow-controlled dungeon territories; deep underground architecture consistent with dark elf lore

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player receives quest from city lords; hiring phase before descent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sewer entrance and initial dungeon levels rendered in 3D; organized architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Manageable monster encounters; party composition and equipment influence success

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dwarf and Drow clan territories visually differentiated; environmental design reflects faction control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple enemy factions present; strategic navigation required to avoid or engage multiple threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Xanathar's lair architecture; culmination of visual dungeon design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounter triggers endgame; upon defeat, narrative conclusion with minimal graphics (DOS version) or animated ending (Amiga version)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blue text window (DOS) or animated sequence (Amiga) depicting return to surface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party treated as heroes; narrative closure signals world-state shift from danger to safety

environmental_storytelling.example: Descent from Waterdeep sewers through dwarf/drow territories to Xanathar's lair communicates escalating threat; deeper = more dangerous
Dwarf and Drow clan levels feature distinct visual architecture reflecting cultural identity and territorial control
Xanathar's cave-in collapse blocks retreat, communicating point-of-no-return and forcing deeper commitment to quest

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: VGA graphics and first-person perspective create 'feeling of being in an actual dungeon' (per critical review)
environmental_storytelling.example: Sound card audio combined with 3D rendering makes environmental danger tactile and immediate

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Vulnerable to larger monster groups; need to recruit NPCs or resurrect skeletons to strengthen
Capable of engaging multiple faction threats; better equipped to handle dungeon challenges
Trigger immediate combat via point-and-click interface; aggressive engagement is default
Available for party recruitment; expand tactical options and party resilience
Boss-level confrontation; final challenge that determines narrative outcome
Encountered in faction-specific dungeon sections; represent organized opposition rather than mindless threats

notes: Eye of the Beholder communicates world-state primarily through environmental design and vertical progression rather than explicit narrative exposition. The shift from surface safety to underground danger is manifested through visual dungeon aesthetics and monster encounter escalation. Faction identity is expressed through territorial occupation of specific dungeon levels rather than explicit visual branding (colors/symbols not documented). The game's 3D perspective and audio design are critical to danger perception—reviewers emphasized the immersive feeling of physical dungeon presence. The endgame shift from danger to safety is marked by the minimal narrative conclusion, contrasting the visceral dungeon experience with a simple text/animation resolution.
